chunk_summary: This section discusses India-specific referral pathways for neurodisability and feeding support through RBSK and DEIC, emphasizing practical strategies for dysphagia management, referral optimization, and addressing feasibility challenges in resource-limited settings.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of RBSK and DEIC in managing neurodisability and feeding challenges in India.
Learn how to write effective referral notes for dysphagia and feeding-related issues.
Identify India-specific barriers to feeding support and strategies to address them.
Explore the integration of child-health programs for accessible therapy and nutrition services.
key_facts: RBSK covers children from birth to 18 years for screening, early intervention, and referral support.
DEICs focus on early childhood services (up to 6 years) and onward linkage to rehabilitation for older children.
Referral notes should include explicit reasons like 'unsafe swallowing with recurrent chest infections' or 'feeding time >3 hours/day with growth faltering.'
India-specific challenges include limited access to commercial thickeners, tube feeding supplies, and caregiver literacy.
School-identified children should be referred via PHC/CHC into the DEIC network to reduce self-navigation burden on families.
reference_quote: Under RBSK, children from birth to 18 years can be screened for selected conditions, linked to confirmation, early intervention, referral support, and tracking. (Source: Operational Guidelines: DEIC-RBSK)
document_key_topics: Role of RBSK and DEIC in feeding support
Addressing feasibility challenges in resource-limited settings
